content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of length MMM, we define f(B)f(B)f(B) as follows:


Initialize score=0\text{score} = 0score=0.
Then, perform the following operation M−1M-1M−1 times:

Choose an index iii (1≤i<∣B∣1 \leq i \lt |B|1≤i<∣B∣).
Add min⁡(Bi,Bi+1)\min(B_i, B_{i+1})min(Bi​,Bi+1​) to score\text{score}score, and then delete max⁡(Bi,Bi+1)\max(B_i, B_{i+1})max(Bi​,Bi+1​) from BBB.

This reduces the length of BBB by exactly 111.


f(B)f(B)f(B) is defined to be the maximum possible value of score\text{score}score at the end of this process.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Process QQQ updates on it.

Each update gives you two integers iii and XXX. You must set Ai=XA_i = XAi​=X, and then compute the value of f(A)f(A)f(A).

Updates to the array are permanent.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the length of the array and the number of updates.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ – the initial elements of AAA.
The next QQQ lines describe the updates. Each of them contains two space-separated integers iii and XXX, meaning you should set Ai=XA_i = XAi​=X.




Output Format
After each update, output on a new line the value of f(A)f(A)f(A).
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases both won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, A=[3,2,1,3]A = [3, 2, 1, 3]A=[3,2,1,3].

The first update sets A1A_1A1​ to 444, so A=[4,2,1,3]A = [4, 2, 1, 3]A=[4,2,1,3] now.
For this array, a score of 444 can be obtained as follows:

Choose i=3i = 3i=3, add min⁡(1,3)=1\min(1, 3) = 1min(1,3)=1 to the score, and then delete 333. The array is [4,2,1][4, 2, 1][4,2,1] now.
Choose i=1i = 1i=1, add min⁡(4,2)=2\min(4, 2) = 2min(4,2)=2 to the score, and delete 444. The array is [2,1][2, 1][2,1].
Choose i=1i = 1i=1, add min⁡(2,1)=1\min(2, 1) = 1min(2,1)=1 to the score, and delete 222. The array is [1][1][1] and the process ends.
The score is 1+2+1=41+2+1 = 41+2+1=4, which is optimal.


After the second update, the array is [4,2,2,3][4, 2, 2, 3][4,2,2,3] which has a score of 666.
After the third update, the array is [4,2,2,1][4, 2, 2, 1][4,2,2,1] which has a score of 555.
